title: [lichen_l2.c:517] lichen_link_rx may write to out_ipv6 even on failure
description:
Final pass: This was missed because reviewers assumed lichen_link_rx follows the pattern of 'don't modify output on failure'.

Root cause: At line 517-518:
  ret = lichen_link_rx(ctx, replay, frame, frame_len, out_ipv6, out_len, src_eui64);

The peer_try_all_pubkeys function iterates through peers, calling lichen_link_rx for each. When signature verification fails (-LICHEN_EAUTH), the code continues to the next peer.

However, lichen_link_rx may have already performed partial work before failing:
1. Parsed the frame header
2. Attempted MIC verification (which failed)
3. Partially decompressed SCHC data into out_ipv6

If lichen_link_rx writes to out_ipv6 before verifying the signature/MIC, then on failure:
- out_ipv6 contains partially decompressed (unauthenticated) data
- On the next peer iteration, out_len is restored but out_ipv6 is NOT zeroed

This means if peer[0] fails with partial data written, and peer[1] succeeds, the success path uses the correctly decompressed data. But if ALL peers fail, out_ipv6 contains unauthenticated data from the last attempted decompression.

The caller (lichen_l2_input) only uses out_ipv6 on success, so this isn't exploitable. But it's a latent risk if the API contract changes.

Fix: Either document that out_ipv6 is undefined on failure, or have peer_try_all_pubkeys zero it on final failure.
close_reason: Fixed: Added secure_zero(out_ipv6) on final failure in peer_try_all_pubkeys to clear partial unauthenticated data
